images.caption: Figure 1:Prompt-based relative importance estimation via text-encoder similarity.Given a target promptppand per-LoRA trigger-word sets𝒦i\mathcal{K}_{i}, we compute a relative importance score for each LoRA.(a) PAWremoves LoRA-iitrigger words𝒦i\mathcal{K}_{i}from the prompt to formp−ip_{-i}and scores importance by semantic changemiPAW=1−cos⁡(c,c−i)m_{i}^{\mathrm{PAW}}=1-\cos(c,c_{-i}).(b) PTWencodes𝒦i\mathcal{K}_{i}and scores alignment with the promptmiPTW=cos⁡(c,cki)m_{i}^{\mathrm{PTW}}=\cos(c,c_{k_{i}}).

Figure 2:Overview of the two prompt-aware multi-LoRA composition methods.W-Compositeaggregates the LoRA-augmented noise predictions at every timestep using fixed, prompt-derived weightswiw_{i}.W-Switchactivates exactly one LoRA per timestep, following a cyclic schedule with within-cycle block lengths proportional towiw_{i}.
Figure 3:𝐈𝐀𝐫𝐜𝐅𝐚𝐜𝐞\mathbf{I_{ArcFace}}vs. the number of composed LoRAs𝐍\mathbf{N}.The dashed line denotes theN=1N=1upper bound. Identity alignment degrades only slightly as more LoRAs are composed.

Figure 4:Qualitative comparison of multi-LoRA composition on theComposLoRAtestbed.Columns show Switch[68], Composite[68], CMLoRA[69]and the proposedW-CompositeandW-Switchmethods. Each row adds one additional LoRA.

Figure 5:CLIP embedding space visualization for cropped embeddings of two generated images,GGandG′G^{\prime}and reference images{Ri}i=15\{R_{i}\}_{i=1}^{5}of a character LoRA. Blue regions denote reference neighborhoods associated with high identity preservation and semantic fidelity.
Figure 6:Example of concept-specific cropped regions extracted from a multi-concept image generated using four concept LoRAs.
